title_en: 1. To restrain the heart from rebelling against and complaining about the fate that
title_ar: ١. كف القلب عن التسخط من القدر
content_en: Allah has ordained and to strive to achieve the station of acceptance and rejoicing in whatever fate brings them. 2. To stop the tongue from showing fright, or uttering what is forbidden, and to strive to achieve the higher station of saying what Allah has instructed us to say, "Innaa lil-laahi wa innaa ilaihi raaji'oon (To Allah we belong, and to Him is our return). [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayah 156.] 3. To restrain the hands from committing acts of disobedience, such as slapping the cheeks and tearing the clothes, as such actions reflect the lack of one's pleasure with Allah's destiny. This is the type of endurance which is legally commendable, and how excellent this provision is! "And Allah loves the patient." [Surah Ale-Imran (3), Ayah 146.]
title_id: 1. Menahan Hati dari Memberontak dan Mengeluh terhadap Takdir
content_id: yang telah Allah ﷻ tetapkan dan berusaha mencapai kedudukan penerimaan dan keridaan terhadap apapun takdir yang datang kepada mereka. 2. Menghentikan lisan dari menunjukkan rasa takut, atau mengucapkan apa yang dilarang, dan berusaha mencapai kedudukan yang lebih tinggi dengan mengucapkan apa yang Allah ﷻ perintahkan kita untuk katakan, "Innaa lillaahi wa innaa ilaihi raaji'uun (Sesungguhnya kami adalah kepunyaan Allah dan kepada Allah jugalah kami kembali)." [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayat 156.] 3. Menahan tangan dari melakukan tindakan kemaksiatan, seperti menampar pipi dan merobek pakaian, karena tindakan seperti itu mencerminkan kurangnya keridhaan seseorang terhadap takdir Allah ﷻ. Inilah jenis kesabaran yang terpuji secara hukum, dan alangkah indahnya bekal ini! "Dan Allah mencintai orang-orang yang sabar." [Surah Ali Imran (3), Ayat 146.]
content_ar: الله has ordained and to strive to achieve the station of acceptance and rejoicing in whatever fate brings them. 2. To stop the tongue from showing fright, or uttering what is forbidden, and to strive to achieve the higher station of saying what الله has instructed us to say, "Innaa lil-laahi wa innaa ilaihi raaji'oon (To الله we belong, and to Him is our return). [Surah Al-Baqarah (2), Ayah 156.] 3. To restrain the hands from committing acts of disobedience, such as slapping the cheeks and tearing the clothes, as such actions reflect the lack of one's pleasure with الله's destiny. This is the type of endurance which is legally commendable, and how excellent this provision is! "And الله loves the patient." [Surah Ale-Imran (3), Ayah 146.]
